claims.claim: Contact Type property enables segmentation of non-prospect contacts (customers, GPO partners, vendors) to prevent them from receiving sales automation and drip campaigns
Lifecycle Stage and Contact Type serve complementary but distinct purposes: Lifecycle Stage tracks position in sales funnel, Contact Type tracks nature of relationship
MQL→SQL automation was using 'is equal to all of' operator on single-select fields, making qualification impossible because users can only select one option at a time
Single-select fields should use radio buttons or dropdown selects rather than checkboxes to enforce one-selection-only at form level
BluePoint ATM website form submissions are not automatically syncing to HubSpot, forcing manual contact entry
Gravity Forms plugin update broke field mapping between website contact forms and HubSpot, preventing automatic contact creation
HubSpot Gmail extension autofill bug causes new contact email field to default to BluePoint's own domain (email@bluepointatm.com) instead of prospect's actual email
Automated notification emails from website contact forms are not being logged in HubSpot activity feed for corresponding contact records
When a contact reaches Opportunity lifecycle stage, a deal should be created automatically via workflow to preserve pipeline visibility during contact turnover
CallRail integration with HubSpot enables automatic contact creation for unknown callers using phone number + '@call.com' as email format
Dynamic number swap for CallRail was not firing on BluePoint ATM live site at time of testing, preventing validation of dynamic-sourced call integration
Large HubSpot databases (10,000+ records) should be segmented using filtered views and bulk-edit operations rather than permanent data restructuring
GCLID tracking is required to connect PPC ad clicks to leads and sales in HubSpot, enabling accurate ROI measurement across campaigns
HubSpot has a confirmed timezone bug causing scheduled social media posts to be set for 9 PM CST instead of 9 AM CST
Fields should be assigned to the object that owns the underlying truth: contact object for person-specific attributes, company object for business-specific attributes
Company-level attributes (juicing status, number of locations, volume estimates) should live on company object so all contacts at that company share consistent data
BluePoint ATM's HubSpot training should focus on daily-use essentials (contact management, note-taking, activity logging, email integration) rather than advanced features
Aviary's HubSpot instance requires upgrade from Sales Hub Professional to Marketing Hub Professional to enable email automation and drip campaigns
BluePoint should consolidate around Lifecycle Stage as single primary metric and retire redundant Lead Status field
HubSpot contact list columns can display company-level name property, not just contact-level company field, to show associated company names correctly
HubSpot sidebar customizations are per-user by default, requiring each team member to configure their views independently
Bulk edits in HubSpot are difficult or impossible to undo automatically with no native rollback functionality
Aviary's Apollo enrichment workflow imported corrupted company names (showing 'Name' column header instead of actual company names) and bloated company list from ~10k to ~15,695 records
Filtering a 10,500-company database to Credit Union + 100+ employees yields approximately 600 records, a workable list for focused outreach
Aviary's top-91 ABM segment lacks data density needed for effective outreach, with many accounts missing phone numbers, org charts, LinkedIn profiles, and role assignments
Contact database cleanup at Citrus America aims to reduce ~8,200 marketing contacts by at least 50% to cut HubSpot subscription costs by ~$20k/year
Sending newsletters to unengaged contacts ('gray matter') hurts deliverability and skews open rates; should switch to engaged-only segment
BANT scoring model simplified for rollout: Budget, Authority, and Need must each score 3; Timing must score 3 or 4

Lifecycle Stage definitions: Subscriber (email only), Lead (minimal profile data), MQL (sufficient profile for outreach but no BANT confirmation), SQL (all BANT confirmed), Opportunity (deal in progress), Customer (closed/won)
SQL criteria set too strictly (requiring full BANT plus additional qualifiers) prevents meaningful lead flow to sales team
HubSpot MQL→SQL automation was broken due to field mismatch (triggered on 'You juicing currently?' instead of 'Are you already juicing?')
Fathom integration with HubSpot is manual per-call (no bulk sync option); requires intentional 'Sync to CRM' action after each call
BluePoint's Product field sync was failing due to mismatch between form-submitted values and HubSpot property options
HubSpot contact database cleanup requires three-step process: export, enrich via Clay, validate via ZeroBounce, then reimport
B2B contact list uploads require field mapping validation and data quality checks (missing emails, phone formatting issues)
SQL workflow can be efficiently built by cloning MQL workflow and adding BANT criteria groups as AND conditions
Simplified BANT scoring (all dimensions = 3, Timing = 3 or 4) drives better team adoption than complex weighted systems
ABM pipeline requires per-company research documents, strategy notes, and target contact mapping stored as custom properties in HubSpot
AviaryAI had zero SQLs generated in initial contract period due to misalignment on lead definitions and pipeline exclusion rules
HubSpot Professional can support multi-funnel architecture (ABM, niche marketing, inbound) with proper MQL/SQL trigger definitions
Contacts marked as 'Not Interested' should remain as marketing contacts and continue receiving newsletters unless they actively unsubscribe
HubSpot data model failures silently break automation rather than surfacing errors, making setup disproportionately consequential
Contact-level attributes belong on Contact object; company-level attributes belong on Company object to prevent data loss when contacts depart
Lifecycle Stage should be the primary funnel metric; Lead Status is redundant and should be retired
Field proliferation (redundant fields accumulating through ad-hoc setup) is a recurring failure mode across clients
BANT qualification framework is dominant across clients but implementation details vary significantly
Simplified BANT approaches outperform nuanced scoring systems because sales teams don't use complex systems consistently
Gravity Forms to HubSpot dropdown mapping requires exact string matches between form choice values and HubSpot property option internal values
Deals should be logged at one month's MRR, not annualized contract value, to keep pipeline realistic
With MRR-based valuation, expect to win roughly one-third to one-half of active deals
Form submission data appears in Activity tab, not Overview tab — this is standard HubSpot behavior, not a tracking failure
Deactivating a HubSpot user without reassigning contacts first causes workflow failures where steps send notifications to the contact owner
HubSpot's social publishing integration with Instagram is subject to intermittent connection failures that cause scheduled posts to silently fail
Contact Type custom property (Lead, Customer, Vendor, Partner) prevents unwanted campaign sends to non-prospect contacts
HubSpot dropdown fields require exact string match to map correctly from form submissions
HubSpot databases accumulated through ad-hoc list imports suffer from poor categorization, stale contact information, and unverified email addresses
Checkboxes are superior to text or dropdown fields for BANT qualification in HubSpot workflow automation
HubSpot's per-seat user limits prevent multiple team members from accessing client accounts simultaneously
BANT fields should be configured as dropdown fields on the Contact record with tiered values indicating qualification strength
HubSpot workflows silently fail; errors accumulate in the 'Needs Review' queue without alerting anyone
The HubSpot API is a high-leverage tool for bulk deduplication, email verification, and enrichment tasks
A 37,000-record database was fully deduplicated via API in approximately 2 minutes
Email verification across a large contact list can be completed via API in ~5 minutes
ZoomInfo credits operate on a strict monthly reset cycle and do not roll over
Fathom's 'Sync to CRM' button is currently a manual one-click action, not fully automatic
CallRail provides attribution tracking, dynamic number insertion, call recording, and AI call analysis
The Aviary ABM email campaign achieved a 37% open rate and 32% click rate as of March 10, 2026
The Aviary ABM campaign sent 238 emails with 77 queued as of March 10, 2026
CallRail call recordings are not available in real-time; webhooks must be configured to fire post-call
Google keyword data in CallRail may be delayed up to 12 hours
The Lifecycle Stage field is often invisible to users because it is not included in the default contact card view
BluePoint PMAX leads are not syncing correctly into HubSpot; the system populates the wrong email address
AI agents with HubSpot API access can consolidate duplicate fields and update bulk data in minutes rather than hours
Personal Access Tokens (PATs) consistently outperform other HubSpot token types for agent-driven API work
Citrus America field consolidation across 9 forms and 2 workflows was completed in a single session using an AI agent
HubSpot should be designated as the single source of truth for lead management to prevent data fragmentation and ensure consistent handoffs between marketing and sales
Deal auto-creation should trigger at the Opportunity lifecycle stage, not at SQL stage, to avoid premature deal creation and pipeline noise
Qualifying data should be stored at the company level with one-directional sync to contact records to preserve data durability when contacts turn over
BANT qualification should be implemented as structured, visible properties in CRM with conditional automation to route unqualified leads into nurture sequences
Form field mapping between website forms and HubSpot properties can fail silently, causing dropdown/select fields to drop during submission while other fields sync correctly

When bulk-selecting records in HubSpot for list actions, clicking 'Select all X records' after checking the top box is required to act on the entire filtered set; default behavior only acts on the current page (100 records)
HubSpot email sending requires CNAME records (hs-* entries) in the sending domain's DNS to enable email tracking and authentication
When users report form submissions are not appearing in a CRM contact list, the root cause is often a contact filter (such as assignment or ownership filters) rather than a broken integration
Email enrichment via third-party services like Hunter.io can recover actionable contact records at scale, but the process identifies duplicates that must be merged to maintain database integrity
Resolving shared CRM login bottlenecks by reassigning unused seats improves team efficiency and data governance by ensuring individual accountability for account activity
Native filtering tools within CRM are more reliable than CSV export/import cycles for data cleanup and list creation, as column mapping errors during import/export create data integrity issues
Automated list-based workflows prevent task overload more effectively than individual task creation in CRM systems
HubSpot automation with behavioral triggers and engagement thresholds can route contacts between nurture sequences and trigger human outreach at scale

SaaS companies often have subscription tier gaps where their current CRM tier lacks marketing automation features required for ABM execution, necessitating explicit upgrade approval before campaign launch
CRM plugin integrations can auto-fill incorrect contact information (e.g., company email instead of prospect email), requiring investigation and manual correction workflows
Contact activity timelines and attribution data provide reliable verification that form integrations are functioning, even when the contact list view appears empty
Lead progression workflows should allow stage-skipping (e.g., Lead → SQL, bypassing MQL) when a sales rep gathers all required data in a single call
Lifecycle stage fields can be set to 'Unknown' to satisfy workflow trigger conditions when information is not yet available
A 'Departed' lifecycle stage should be used when a known contact leaves their company, preserving the record with full activity history while flagging them as inactive
Deal auto-creation should populate only minimal fields (deal name, deal owner) at creation time, leaving deal type, pipeline priority, amount, and next steps blank for manual entry

When defining SQL criteria via HubSpot segments, a data audit should be run first to validate that fields are sufficiently populated; strict criteria against sparse data results in zero qualifying contacts
Separating audience definition (via dynamic segments) from automation actions (via workflows) creates more maintainable and reusable HubSpot automations
Deal creation should not automatically cancel ABM email sequences for all contacts at an account; instead, cancellation should trigger only when a contact reaches the Opportunity lifecycle stage
In multi-business-unit accounts (e.g., large credit unions), creating a deal for one contact should not stop outreach to other contacts at the same organization
HubSpot data hygiene and segmentation should be completed before campaign launch to enable targeted, ICP-specific campaigns across multiple channels

Redundant lead source fields (e.g., 'Inbound Lead' alongside 'Lead Source' and 'Lead Source Detail') should be consolidated and hidden after data migration to prevent duplicate data entry and confusion
Trade show lead source values should be normalized to a standardized format (e.g., '[ABBREVIATION] [YEAR]') across all contacts for consistency
HubSpot requires at least one seat at the super admin level at all times to avoid being blocked on automation work
HubSpot supports tracking downloads when files are hosted within its file system, providing visibility into whether a prospect has opened or downloaded a document
Custom AWS SES-based email automation with HubSpot webhook logging can replace manual email sending and avoid the cost of paid bulk email tools like SendGrid
AWS SES email automation should log the full email body to HubSpot's activity feed via webhooks, not just send/open/click metadata, to provide complete visibility for stakeholders
ABM email sequences should use a 30-day cadence with early emails spaced closer together (days 0, 4, 9) to establish presence, then weekly intervals for later sends
All contact emails should be validated before ABM campaign send; undeliverable addresses should be deleted from HubSpot rather than left in place to avoid future deliverability problems
A 2% unsubscribe rate on the first cold email send is considered excellent and a positive indicator of personalization value
HubSpot's native email tooling lacks the per-account personalization required for true ABM; third-party platforms like Orbit should be used for ABM sending with activity synced back to HubSpot

patterns.pattern: Form sync failures are recurring issue across multiple clients (BluePoint ATM, Citrus America) — WordPress/Gravity Forms integrations breaking after plugin updates or misconfiguration
HubSpot integration issues often stem from permission/access gaps — team members lacking Super Admin seats cannot investigate or remediate integration settings
Large contact database imports (Apollo, bulk uploads) create data quality issues: corrupted field values, bloated record counts, undifferentiated segmentation
Redundant or overlapping fields (Lead Status + Lifecycle Stage, multiple contact type fields) create confusion about which field to update and report on
Automation trigger logic errors occur when operators (e.g., 'is equal to all of') are mismatched to field types (single-select vs. multi-select)
Contact-level vs. company-level field assignment is a recurring design decision — fields should live on the object that owns the underlying truth
HubSpot training needs are practical and focused on daily-use features rather than advanced capabilities — clients resist async video courses and prefer hands-on sessions
Email integration and logging issues are common: Gmail sync bugs, form notification emails not being logged, auto-reply delays
HubSpot tier upgrades are often required to unlock marketing automation features (Marketing Hub Pro for email campaigns, drip sequences)
Database hygiene and contact cleanup are necessary prerequisites for effective CRM operations — large unqualified contact lists hurt deliverability and inflate costs
Field mapping mismatches between forms and HubSpot properties cause silent sync failures; exact string matching is required
B2B clients entering HubSpot for first time lack defined MQL/SQL criteria; this must be established before automation can function
Contact data cleanup is a prerequisite for reliable outreach; missing emails, invalid addresses, and stale records block campaign execution
ABM campaigns require per-company research and strategy documents stored in CRM properties to enable personalized outreach at scale
HubSpot training and onboarding is a recurring need for new clients; estimated 1 hour for functional walkthrough
Contact card cleanup (hiding unused fields) improves sales rep experience without deleting underlying data
Multi-funnel architecture (ABM, inbound, paid, events) requires separate pipeline tracks with distinct lifecycle stages and automation
Checkbox fields are preferred for workflow criteria evaluation (BANT qualification) over text/dropdown fields due to unambiguous true/false logic
Clients accumulate redundant fields through ad-hoc setup, creating duplicate data entry and automation ambiguity
Form integration failures are typically caused by field value mismatches between form options and HubSpot property values
HubSpot setup requires significant upfront structural decisions that have long-term consequences for automation and reporting
Contact capacity management requires post-campaign pruning of non-engaging contacts to free slots for new imports
Sales team adoption of HubSpot workflows depends on reducing friction in data entry — standard contact profile is cumbersome
HubSpot user permission issues are common root causes of missing activity data; often a permission toggle, not a data problem
Deal records should contain pitch decks, call recordings, and activity logs as attachments for single source of truth
BANT qualification fields are used to automate the transition of contacts to SQL status and trigger deal creation
Lifecycle stage progression follows a consistent funnel: Prospect → Lead → MQL → SQL → Opportunity → Customer
Contact Type and Lifecycle Stage are two distinct fields that should not be conflated; Contact Type captures role/relationship, Lifecycle Stage captures sales progression
HubSpot workflow errors are common and require systematic review; deactivated users are a frequent root cause
Data enrichment and verification should happen before re-import to protect sender reputation and improve contact quality
Multi-system integrations (HubSpot + CallRail + Fathom) are used to close attribution gaps between phone calls and digital activity
ABM campaigns in HubSpot use account-level tracking with custom company properties (ABM Tier, Vertical, Campaign, Research Status, Outreach Status)
Form integration issues result in manual data entry and broken automation workflows
Shared team logins are proposed as a solution to HubSpot per-seat user limits across multiple client accounts
Lead qualification workflows require multiple sequential stages (Prospect → Lead → MQL → SQL → Opportunity) with explicit field requirements at each stage
BANT (Budget, Authority, Need, Timing) is a standard qualification framework implemented across multiple clients as the trigger for SQL/Opportunity stage advancement
Form field mapping issues (missing fields, incorrect field types, misconfigured dropdowns) are a recurring integration problem between website forms and HubSpot
HubSpot access and permissions management is critical; loss of super admin access blocks automation work and requires immediate remediation

ABM campaigns require a decision between native HubSpot email automation and third-party platforms (Apollo, SendGrid, Orbit, AWS SES) based on cost, personalization needs, and integration requirements
Redundant or legacy fields accumulate in HubSpot over time and require consolidation and cleanup to prevent data entry confusion and maintain database integrity
Deal auto-creation workflows should be triggered by lifecycle stage progression (Opportunity) rather than other events (SQL, deal creation) to avoid premature or duplicate deal creation
HubSpot lifecycle stages should include non-active stages (Departed, Unqualified, Not Ready) to handle contacts who exit the active pipeline while preserving data history
Dynamic segments (formerly lists) should be used to define audience qualification criteria separately from workflow actions, enabling reusability and easier maintenance
Data quality audits should precede automation implementation; sparse field population can result in zero qualifying contacts and wasted automation effort
ABM campaigns require careful trigger logic to avoid unintended side effects in multi-business-unit accounts; deal creation for one contact should not stop outreach to others at the same organization
Email automation platforms should log full email body content to HubSpot activity feeds for stakeholder visibility, not just engagement metrics
Lead handoff processes require explicit notification protocols (email, Slack) and a single source of truth (HubSpot) to ensure fast sales follow-up

contradictions.claim_a: BluePoint ATM website form submissions are not automatically syncing to HubSpot
contradictions.claim_b: HubSpot shows the Gravity Form as a non-HubSpot form with 57 recorded submissions, indicating the form is partially connected
contradictions.note: Not a direct contradiction — submissions are being logged in HubSpot but not creating contact records. The form is partially connected at a surface level but not configured to create/update contacts.

contradictions.claim_a: SQL criteria should require full BANT plus additional qualifiers to ensure high-quality leads
contradictions.claim_b: SQL criteria should be loosened to focus on two core questions: fit/application match and demonstrated intent, allowing earlier sales engagement
contradictions.context: Citrus America's SQL definition was revised mid-engagement after discovering the strict criteria prevented meaningful lead flow

contradictions.claim_a: Disqualified contacts ('Not Interested') should be removed from marketing communications
contradictions.claim_b: Contacts marked as 'Not Interested' should remain as marketing contacts and continue receiving newsletters unless they actively unsubscribe
contradictions.context: Citrus America explicitly chose to keep disqualified contacts in marketing flows

contradictions.claim_a: Lifecycle Stage should be the single source of truth for sales funnel progression
contradictions.claim_b: Lead Status was being actively used to track sales progress at BluePoint before the decision to retire it
contradictions.note: Not a true contradiction — rather a documented transition from one approach to another. Both were in use simultaneously, creating confusion.

contradictions.claim_a: BANT fields should be configured as checkboxes for clean boolean logic in workflow automation
contradictions.claim_b: BANT fields should be configured as dropdown fields with tiered values indicating qualification strength
contradictions.note: These articles describe different BANT field structures. The checkbox approach is recommended for Citrus America; the dropdown approach is described as a general pattern. This may reflect different client implementations or a shift in best practice.

contradictions.claim_a: Deal auto-creation should trigger at the SQL stage
contradictions.claim_b: Deal auto-creation should trigger at the Opportunity stage, not SQL, to avoid premature deal creation and pipeline noise
contradictions.source_b: wiki/knowledge/hubspot/citrus-america-deal-auto-creation-workflow.md (November 2025 update: 'replaced an earlier workflow that triggered deal creation at the SQL stage')
contradictions.resolution: The contradiction reflects an evolution in best practice. The SQL-stage trigger was identified as creating noise and was deactivated in November 2025 in favor of the Opportunity-stage trigger.

contradictions.claim_a: ABM email sequences should be cancelled when a deal is created for a contact
contradictions.source_a: wiki/knowledge/hubspot/aviary-abm-automation-workflow.md (current behavior: 'workflow cancels email sequences for a contact as soon as a HubSpot deal is created')
contradictions.claim_b: ABM email sequences should not be cancelled for all contacts at an account when a deal is created; cancellation should trigger only when a contact reaches the Opportunity lifecycle stage
contradictions.source_b: wiki/knowledge/hubspot/aviary-abm-automation-workflow.md (proposed solution: 'Change the cancellation trigger from deal creation to the Opportunity lifecycle stage')
contradictions.resolution: The contradiction reflects a discovered flaw in the current implementation. The proposed solution (Opportunity-stage trigger) is more precise and avoids unintended cancellation of sequences for other contacts at multi-business-unit accounts.

exceptions.general_rule: Contact Type property should exclude non-leads from sales automation and drip campaigns
exceptions.exception: Vendors are included in HubSpot despite being excluded from marketing automation, to allow email history to be tracked centrally

exceptions.general_rule: Lifecycle Stage automation should be bidirectional (contacts can move forward and backward through stages)
exceptions.exception: Disqualified stage was added as a terminal stage for contacts who should never be re-engaged (e.g., 'do not call' requests)

exceptions.general_rule: Bulk edits in HubSpot should be tested on small subsets first due to lack of rollback functionality
exceptions.exception: Full database cleanup was greenlit at Citrus America without explicit mention of testing on subset first, though hygiene run is being done in phases

exceptions.general_rule: HubSpot integrations should automatically sync data between systems
exceptions.exception: Fathom integration requires manual per-call 'Sync to CRM' action; no bulk or automatic sync option available

exceptions.general_rule: Lifecycle stage progression should be automated via workflows once criteria are met
exceptions.exception: AnChain.ai's high-touch, low-volume sales motion requires deliberate manual progression gates rather than automated bulk advancement
exceptions.context: Niche B2B sales with small deal volume

exceptions.general_rule: Deprecated field values should be removed from contact records during cleanup
exceptions.exception: Citrus America chose to migrate deprecated Market Segment values to 'Other' rather than delete them, preserving data integrity

exceptions.general_rule: Contact-level attributes should not be stored on Contact object if they are company-specific
exceptions.exception: Citrus America stores qualifying data at company level but syncs it down to contact records for sales rep visibility
exceptions.context: B2B sales operations where reps need quick access to company-level data without navigating to separate company record

exceptions.general_rule: Contacts should be deleted when no longer relevant to prevent CRM bloat
exceptions.exception: Old Full Name fields should be hidden, not deleted, to preserve historical entry data
exceptions.context: Form field restructuring where historical data must be retained

exceptions.general_rule: All contacts should be marked as marketing contacts when imported
exceptions.exception: Contacts should be imported as non-marketing contacts first, then promoted to marketing status when a campaign is ready to send
exceptions.context: Contact capacity management on limited tier plans

exceptions.general_rule: All four BANT criteria must be met before a contact qualifies for SQL status
exceptions.exception: Citrus America's SQL workflow uses an OR logic — a contact becomes SQL when they answer ANY ONE of ~10 sales qualification questions, not when all BANT fields are confirmed

exceptions.general_rule: Lifecycle Stage field is visible on all contact records by default
exceptions.exception: The Lifecycle Stage field is often invisible to users because it is not included in the default contact card view; admins must manually add it

exceptions.general_rule: Fathom call recordings sync automatically to HubSpot
exceptions.exception: Fathom's 'Sync to CRM' button is currently a manual one-click action, not fully automatic; each team member must enable the sync in their own Fathom account settings
ZoomInfo credits operate on a strict monthly reset cycle and do not roll over; credits not used within the calendar month are forfeited

exceptions.general_rule: Lead progression should follow the standard sequence: Prospect → Lead → MQL → SQL → Opportunity
exceptions.exception: If a sales rep gathers all MQL and SQL data in a single call, the contact may advance directly from Lead → SQL, bypassing the MQL stage

exceptions.general_rule: Form field mapping should sync all form fields to HubSpot automatically
exceptions.exception: Dropdown/select fields may fail to sync while other fields (name, email, phone, company) sync correctly, requiring investigation of field mapping configuration

exceptions.general_rule: Deal auto-creation should happen automatically when qualification criteria are met
exceptions.exception: Manual deal creation is still available at any stage; sales reps can create a deal immediately if a call warrants it, bypassing the automation

exceptions.general_rule: All contacts at an account should be treated as a single pipeline unit
exceptions.exception: In multi-business-unit accounts (e.g., large credit unions), different business units may be at different stages of the sales process and should receive independent outreach

exceptions.general_rule: HubSpot's native email tooling should be used for all email campaigns
exceptions.exception: ABM campaigns requiring per-account personalization (unique research reports, strategy documents, individually personalized copy) should use third-party platforms like Orbit with activity synced back to HubSpot

evidence.context: Website form submissions create HubSpot contacts successfully for name, email, phone, company, but the Product/Service dropdown selection is dropped entirely

evidence.metric: Deal auto-creation workflow deactivated after creating noise in the pipeline
evidence.context: The old SQL-stage workflow (created April 2024) was deactivated in November 2025 after it was identified as the source of premature deal creation

evidence.metric: 13-criteria SQL definition with sparse field population
evidence.context: The 'Are you already juicing?' field had zero matches across ~10,000 contacts; implementing all 13 criteria as-is would have produced an empty SQL list

evidence.metric: ABM email sequences cancelled unintentionally for multi-business-unit accounts
evidence.context: Creating a deal for one contact at a large credit union caused sequences for other contacts at the same account to be cancelled, stopping outreach that should continue

evidence.metric: Redundant lead source field accumulation
evidence.context: Three lead source fields existed simultaneously (Inbound Lead, Lead Source, Lead Source Detail), causing duplicate data entry and confusion during MQL/SQL automation setup

evidence.metric: HubSpot access removal blocking automation work
evidence.context: Chris (Asymmetric) was inadvertently removed from super admin status, blocking progress on pending email automation updates including the stadium automation campaign

evidence.metric: ABM email platform decision blocking campaign launch
evidence.context: Outreach to the top 100 tier-1 accounts cannot begin until the choice between HubSpot upgrade and Apollo/SendGrid is made and the platform is configured

evidence.metric: ABM campaign initial performance: 13% open rate, 11% click rate, 2% bounce rate, 2% unsubscribe rate
evidence.context: First send to 101 contacts; 2% unsubscribe rate on email 1 considered excellent for cold outreach

evidence.metric: 104 Tier 1 accounts in ABM campaign with one primary contact per company
evidence.context: One-to-one account mapping with personalized research reports, strategy documents, and 6-email sequence per account

evidence.metric: Lead handoff notification protocol established with explicit channels
evidence.context: Primary channel: email to Aaron Grossman; secondary channel: Slack; manual notification required to ensure fast follow-up

evidence.metric: Modified BANT framework in use (Budget, Authority, Need — Timeline de-emphasized)
evidence.context: Timeline is de-emphasized given how new the product category is; a 'good' first call results in the prospect opening the next meeting to a larger internal audience
